education.description: Biomedical Engineering - Neuroscience/NeuroEngineering; Adviser: Ross K. Maddox; Thesis: Neural Encoding of Musical Acoustics and Higher Order Features Across the Human Auditory System
Data Science Boot Camp; Deep Learning Boot Camp

experience.title: Postdoctoral Research Scientist
experience.institution: Stanford University, Department of Psychiatry & Behavioral Sciences, Stanford Medicine
experience.description: Lead independent and collaborative research on neural mechanisms of speech communication and social interaction; Conduct NIH-funded studies on speaker-listener brain coupling and auditory temporal integration during naturalistic communication in children with autism spectrum disorder (ASD); Initiated and developed a new EEG research program for pediatric auditory neuroscience

experience.title: Research Scientist Intern - Audio Team
experience.description: Led end-to-end research project to improve auditory experiences in AR/VR wearable devices through neurophysiological signal analysis on prototype hardware; Independently designed and executed the full experiment pipeline—stimulus design, data collection, signal processing, and ML-based data analysis—to evaluate hearing health in real-world environments; Developed Python pipelines for processing noisy neurophysiological data from wearable sensors

experience.title: Graduate Researcher
experience.description: Developed novel computational methods to derive human auditory brainstem responses from continuous naturalistic stimuli; Engineered chimeric music stimuli and applied EEG/ERP/TRF and ML decoding to investigate neural encoding of pitch-timing interactions in music cognition; Designed and evaluated speech-in-noise studies with DNN-generated talking face stimuli

experience.title: Research Assistant
experience.institution: Huaxi MR Research Center (HMRRC), Sichuan University, China
experience.description: Gathered, analyzed, and processed data in functional MRI & Diffusion Tensor Imaging (DTI); Conducted research quantifying white matter changes through DTI in earthquake survivors and healthy controls

experience.title: Teaching Assistant
experience.description: Provided support to courses including BME 230: Signals, Systems & Imaging and BME 220: Biomedical Computation & Statistics

skills: Python (PyTorch, scikit-learn, NumPy, SciPy, MNE, Pandas)

memberships.name: Association for Research in Otolaryngology (ARO)
memberships.description: Member of ARO Education Committee. Organize professional workshops, seminar series and coffee hours.

memberships.name: Organization for Women in Science for the Developing World
